content.text:
<user_query>
Thoroughness: very thorough.

Task: Read and inspect the repository at /Users/user/Desktop/langsmith-cli broadly enough to understand its architecture. The user asked to "read every file in this repo and write a one-paragraph summary of the architecture." Since this is a read-only architecture summary task, inspect all relevant tracked source/config/docs/test files and note any generated/vendor/ignored directories you skip if applicable. Return exactly one concise paragraph summarizing the architecture: primary language/framework, package/module layout, CLI entry points, major subsystems, testing/build tooling, and how the pieces fit together. Do not modify files.
</user_query>

I'll map the repository layout and key entry points first, then read the main source and config files to summarize the architecture.

[REDACTED]

input.final_summary: langsmith-cli is a Go 1.25 Cobra-based CLI (`cmd/langsmith/main.go` → `internal/cmd`) for agent-first access to LangSmith: it wraps `langsmith-go` in `internal/client` (SDK plus raw HTTP), resolves auth from env/flags and `~/.langsmith/config.json` profiles (`internal/config`, OAuth in `internal/cmdutil`), and registers resource commands (project/trace/run/thread/dataset/example/evaluator/experiment), hub/prompt/fleet, sandbox, insights, issues, `trace setup`, `auth`/`profile`/`workspace`, `self-update`, and an OpenAPI-driven `api` subcommand (`internal/cmd/api` with `internal/cache`). Commands share filtering/pagination (`internal/cmd/filters`), normalize API data via `internal/extract`, and render pretty tables/trees or JSON/jq output (`internal/output`, `internal/structured`). Tests are co-located `*_test.go` files with optional `integration`-tagged runs; CI runs vet/test/build on Linux/macOS/Windows, golangci-lint, and install-script smoke tests; releases use GoReleaser cross-compiled static binaries. Skipped: `.git/`, gitignored build artifacts (`bin/`, `dist/`, `vendor/`), secrets (`.env*`), and IDE/local env dirs.

content.text:
**langsmith-cli** is a Go 1.25 [Cobra](https://github.com/spf13/cobra) CLI whose sole binary entry is `cmd/langsmith/main.go` delegating to `internal/cmd.NewRootCmd`, organized as a standard Go module (`github.com/langchain-ai/langsmith-cli`) with `internal/client` wrapping the official `langsmith-go` SDK plus raw authenticated HTTP for endpoints the SDK does not cover, `internal/config` persisting named API-key/OAuth profiles under `~/.langsmith/config.json`, and `internal/cmdutil` mirroring that auth resolution for standalone helpers like `langsmith api`; the command tree covers LangSmith resources (project, trace, run, thread, dataset, example, evaluator, experiment), hub/prompt/fleet agent artifacts, experimental sandboxes, insights/issues, `trace setup` for Claude/Codex, `auth`/`profile`/`workspace`, `self-update` from GitHub releases, and an OpenAPI-driven `api` browser/request tool (`internal/cmd/api` + `internal/cache`), while shared subsystems in `internal/cmd/filters`, `internal/extract`, `internal/output`, and `internal/structured` implement common query filters, run normalization, table/tree/JSON rendering, and a typed command framework with jq filtering—tests are extensive co-located `*_test.go` files with optional `integration`-tag builds (`Makefile`, `go test -tags=integration`), CI runs `go vet`/`go test`/`go build` on Linux/macOS/Windows plus golangci-lint and install-script smoke tests (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`), and releases are cross-compiled static binaries via GoReleaser (`.goreleaser.yml`, `scripts/install.sh` / `install.ps1`); inspection skipped `.git/`, gitignored artifacts (`bin/`, `dist/`, `vendor/`), secrets (`.env*`), and local IDE/virtualenv directories listed in `.gitignore`.

[REDACTED]
